639.reason: // The stack capacity is MAX (101), so valid indices are 0 to 100. The condition should be `S->top == MAX - 1` to check if full.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  // The stack capacity is MAX (101), so valid indices are 0 to 100. The condition should be `S->top == MAX - 1` to check if full.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition should be `S->top == MAX - 1` because the stack has MAX=101 elements (indices 0 to 100). When top is 99 (MAX-2), there's still room for one more element. The stack becomes full when top == 100 (i.e., MAX-1).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  // The stack capacity is MAX (101), so valid indices are 0 to 100. The condition should be `S->top == MAX - 1` to check if full. Using `MAX-2` incorrectly allows one extra push beyond capacity.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  // The stack capacity is MAX (101), so valid indices are 0 to 100. The condition should be `S->top == MAX - 1` to check if full. Using `MAX-2` allows pushing one extra element beyond intended capacity.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [The stack capacity is MAX (101), so valid indices are 0 to 100. The condition should be S->top == MAX - 1 to detect full stack. Using MAX-2 allows pushing one extra element beyond intended capacity.]

663.reason: // The loop condition is incorrect. `scanf` returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. It should check for `jud != -1` after reading, or use `while (1)` and break on `jud == -1`.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  // The loop condition is incorrect. `scanf` returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. It should check for `jud != -1` after reading, or use `while (1)` and break on `jud == -1`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop condition `scanf(...) != -1` is incorrect. `scanf` returns the number of successfully read items, not EOF directly. It should check for `== 1` or compare with EOF properly. Also, the problem says input ends with -1 as an operation, not as a scanf failure. This causes infinite loop or wrong termination.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  // The loop condition is incorrect. `scanf` returns the number of successfully read items (1 here), not the value read. It should continue until `jud == -1`. Also, when input ends with -1, this loop won't stop correctly because `scanf` returns 1 even when reading -1, so condition `!= -1` is always true.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  // The loop condition is incorrect. `scanf` returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. It should continue until `jud == -1`, but this condition will never break on input `-1` because `scanf` returns 1 (success), not -1. Also, the problem says `-1` terminates the sequence, so the loop should break when `jud == -1`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. It should be compared to 1 (or >0), not -1. Also, the loop never checks for -1 operation to terminate as per problem description.]
